extra.message: Detected user input used to manually construct a SQL string. This is usually bad practice because manual construction could accidentally result in a SQL injection. An attacker could use a SQL injection to steal or modify contents of the database. Instead, use a parameterized query which is available by default in most database engines. Alternatively, consider using an object-relational mapper (ORM) such as Sequelize which will protect your queries.

extra.message: sqlalchemy.text passes the constructed SQL statement to the database mostly unchanged. This means that the usual SQL injection protections are not applied and this function is vulnerable to SQL injection if user input can reach here. Use normal SQLAlchemy operators (such as or_, and_, etc.) to construct SQL.
